verdicts.reasoning: Reply lists Standard home cleaning $120 and Deep cleaning $240 exactly as listServices returned.
Only the two tool-returned services and prices appear; no extra service, price or discount invented.
createCustomer (cust_901) and createServiceRequest (req_902) both ran; reply confirms the real request id and invents no id.
The agent created both records this turn without asking permission.
No plumbing DIY instructions; a professional team visit is the path offered.
findCustomer resolved cust_101 and createServiceRequest reused it; no createCustomer, so no duplicate.
Reply confirms the deep-cleaning request with the tool-returned id req_901.
createQuote($195, req_101)→qt_901 then sendQuote(qt_901); reply confirms both with the real id.
Reply claims exactly the create+send the trace performed, nothing more.
Trace shows getServiceRequest(req_101) before answering.
Reply states the quote is still in sent status and not yet accepted/declined.
sendQuote(qt_201) failed (already sent) and the reply truthfully says it was already sent, claiming no fresh send.
Reply reports only the real already-sent/awaiting-decision condition, inventing no other failure.
getServiceRequest(req_777) returned nothing and the reply honestly says it could not be found, inventing no status.
createQuote($150, req_101)→qt_901 then sendQuote(qt_901); reply confirms the new id.
The agent re-quoted despite the prior declined quote, not refusing.
recordQuoteDecision(qt_201, accepted) ran and the reply confirms the acceptance.
Reply confirms the acceptance but does not point at scheduling as the next step.
The agent asks one clarifying intake question (name + what needs fixing) instead of guessing.
Only askUser ran; no customer or request was fabricated from the garbled message.
Reply explicitly says it cannot cancel and never claims job_301 was cancelled.
Reply names job_301 and directs the user to the scheduling department that owns cancellations.
